description: Fix WhatsApp Router feedback loop and duplicate message protection

context.summary: Router workflow triggers on ALL message_create events including bot's own messages, causing infinite feedback loop. User's WhatsApp number received 200+ duplicate messages.

design.problem: When the bot sends a message, WhatsApp fires message_create with fromMe:true. This triggers the Router workflow again, which triggers 'Send a text message' node again, causing infinite loop.
design.root_cause: Extract Routing Data node passes all events through without filtering fromMe:true messages
design.solution: Two-layer protection: (1) Filter fromMe:true on message_create events, (2) Add deduplication using workflow static data to prevent same message to same chat within cooldown window

implementation.layer1_fromMe_filter: In Extract Routing Data, return [] when dataType === 'message_create' && fromMe === true
implementation.layer2_deduplication: Use $getWorkflowStaticData('global') to track messageKey (chatId:content) with timestamp, skip if within 60s cooldown

tasks: Update Extract Routing Data Code node to filter fromMe:true on message_create events
Add deduplication logic using workflow static data (chatId + message content + 60s cooldown)
Add cleanup logic to remove old entries from static data (prevent memory growth)
Test: Send message TO bot, verify single response (no loop)
Test: Bot sends message, verify no loop triggered
Update local n8n-workflows/whatsapp-router.json with fixed code
verification: Send WhatsApp message to bot number
wwebjs-api logs show no repeated 'Used Fanout Flow' messages
Workflow execution count matches message count (no infinite executions)
Router workflow can be safely activated without risk of feedback loop
changelog: Fixed feedback loop in WhatsApp Router workflow with fromMe filter and message deduplication
